questions.question: What is the core problem with trying to sell to 'everyone'?

options.text: It costs too much money in marketing
You cannot write messaging that resonates deeply with any specific group
Your product becomes too complex
You need to hire too many salespeople

questions.explanation: When targeting everyone, your messaging becomes generic. Cold outreach only works when the message resonates 100% with the recipient—you cannot write a message that resonates with a 22-year-old YouTuber AND a 50-year-old CTO.

questions.question: What are the THREE hidden costs of poor targeting mentioned in this lesson?

options.text: Hiring costs, software costs, office costs
The Outreach Tax, The Feedback Debt, and Diluted Positioning
Time, money, and reputation
Lead costs, conversion costs, support costs

questions.explanation: The three hidden costs are: (1) Outreach Tax (wasteful outreach), (2) Feedback Debt (wrong product direction), and (3) Diluted Positioning (commoditization).

questions.question: What is the 'Golden Segment' in terms of ICP targeting?

options.text: The largest possible market segment
The segment with the highest pain, easiest access, and where you have right-to-win
Enterprise companies with big budgets
The segment recommended by investors

questions.explanation: The Golden Segment is your Beachhead Market—where pain is high (they need you NOW), access is easy (you can find their emails), and budget is available (they pay for tools).

questions.question: What does 'Diluted Positioning' signal to potential buyers?

options.text: That you are an expert specialist
That you are a commodity with no unique value
That you have a large total addressable market
That your product is enterprise-ready

questions.explanation: Generic positioning involves broad claims like 'we save you time' which signals you are a commodity. Specific positioning signals expertise and relevance.

questions.question: Why is 'The Feedback Debt' so dangerous for product-led founders?

options.text: Because it makes the code base messy
Because it forces you to build features for people who will never make you rich
Because bad reviews are hard to delete
Because non-technical users are harder to support

questions.explanation: If you sell to the wrong people, they give you the wrong feature requests. This pulls your product roadmap away from the high-value 'Golden Segment' and toward low-ROI work.

questions.question: Think about your current customer base or target market. Describe ONE specific segment that you believe could be your 'Golden Segment' and explain why they have high pain, easy access, and available budget.
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if this reflection describes a specific, viable Golden Segment. Check if they address: (1) a specific pain point, (2) how they would access this segment, (3) evidence of budget availability. Provide constructive feedback on how to sharpen their ICP focus.
